exploration_log.aha_moment: Primitive class literals (int.class, long.class, etc.) are not assignable from their boxed wrappers via `isAssignableFrom`. The fix is simply to add `|| primitiveClass == type` checks alongside each existing boxed check.

alternatives: Map primitive classes to boxed classes first, then check isAssignableFrom
Add a separate block for primitives before the existing checks
key_decisions.decision: Inline `|| primitive.class == type` in each existing condition
key_decisions.reasoning: Minimal change, keeps the parallel structure of existing checks, easy to review.

search_sequence.reasoning: Understand the existing structure of isAssignableFrom checks to know where to add primitive mappings
search_sequence.result: Found 6 wrapper type checks, each returning the corresponding Serde. Simple pattern: add parallel primitive check for each.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: serdeFrom
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added primitive class checks (int.class, long.class, etc.) alongside boxed type checks

solve_notes: Added `|| primitiveClass == type` conditions to each existing `isAssignableFrom` check in `serdeFrom(Class<T>)` for all 6 primitive types (int, long, double, float, short, boolean), mapping them to their corresponding boxed Serde instances.
task_text: Fix `Serdes.serdeFrom(Class<T> type)` in `common/serialization/Serdes.java` to handle Java primitive type class literals (`int.class`, `long.class`, `double.class`, `float.class`, `short.class`, `boolean.class`). Extend to map each primitive class to its corresponding boxed-type serde.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set has only the edited `serdeFrom()` method. No additional context is needed since all the primitive-to-boxed mappings are trivially inferred from the existing boxed checks, and no other class needs to be read.
